title: quercus sinuata subsp. breviloba
intro: Quercus sinuata subsp. breviloba, also known as the Mexican white oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with the bark and leaves being the primary parts utilized for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Quercus sinuata subsp. breviloba include its potential to aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as its antioxidant properties which can help protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Quercus sinuata subsp. breviloba can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the bark and leaves typically being dried and then combined with other herbs for medicinal use.
